_note: K5 relay-enforcement scenario triples (journal state, final message, expected verdict). Every contradiction kind in relay_audit_stop._CONTRADICTION_KINDS is covered for BOTH the run relay (verify_relay) and the notebook relay (verify_notebook_relay), plus the pass cases and a fail-open case. `mismatch_kinds` pins every kind the reference verdict carries (incl. non-blocking `unverifiable`); `blocks` is DERIVED in relay_fixtures.py from the hook's own blocking set, never stored here. Notebook seeds use the two-section source in relay_fixtures.NB_SOURCE.

triples.name: run_number_contradiction
triples.doc: number — a wrong core-hours count vs the recorded value
triples.final_message: Run run-1 has failed. It consumed 256 core-hours across 10 tasks.

triples.name: run_state_contradiction
triples.final_message: Run run-1 is still running; results soon.

triples.name: run_run_id_contradiction
triples.doc: run_id — a run-id-like token matching no journaled id
triples.final_message: Run run-1 is done. Results for run-2 are also ready.

triples.name: run_job_id_contradiction
triples.doc: run_id — a job-id-shaped digit run not among the recorded job ids
triples.final_message: Run run-1 is in flight; scheduler job 99999999 handled it.

triples.name: run_zero_count_contradiction
triples.doc: number — 'no failed waves' over a record that counted failures (count claim)
triples.final_message: Run run-1: all good, no failed waves.

triples.name: run_faithful_pass
triples.doc: PASS — a faithful relay: every number/state/id matches the record
triples.final_message: Run run-1 has failed. It consumed 128 core-hours across 10 tasks; the canary was green.

triples.name: run_truncation_pass
triples.doc: PASS — a truncation-prefix decimal (3.14 of 3.1411) is tolerated
triples.final_message: Run run-1 is complete: the metric is 3.14.

triples.name: run_unverifiable_pass
triples.doc: PASS — a numeric claim with no comparable source is unverifiable, not a contradiction (the seam does not block)

triples.name: run_no_claim_pass
triples.doc: PASS — a message naming no run and carrying only conversational numbers
triples.final_message: The setup is proceeding; check back in ~2 minutes.

triples.name: nb_status_contradiction
triples.doc: state (notebook) — a section relayed as auto_cleared while it reduces to unsigned
triples.final_message: For demo-audit, the load-data section is auto-cleared, ready to go.

triples.name: nb_passed_contradiction
triples.doc: state (notebook) — the module 'passed' verdict claimed while a required section is unsigned
triples.final_message: The demo-audit graduation gate passed; ready to submit.

triples.name: nb_sha_contradiction
triples.doc: number (notebook) — a sha-hex matching neither the current nor a recorded sign-off sha
triples.final_message: For demo-audit, section load-data was signed at ffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff.

triples.name: nb_faithful_pass
triples.doc: PASS — both required sections signed and relayed as signed_current
triples.final_message: For demo-audit, load-data is signed_current and fit-model is signed_current.

triples.name: nb_unverifiable_pass
triples.doc: PASS — an unresolvable .py source makes a status claim unverifiable, not a contradiction (the seam drops it)
triples.final_message: For demo-audit, the load-data section is auto_cleared.

triples.name: run_torn_record_failopen
triples.doc: FAIL-OPEN — a torn (non-JSON) run record degrades to unverifiable; the same 'running' claim that blocks with an intact record does NOT block here
